div.head: The Chargé in Germany (Poole) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 202. My 197, September 28, 1 p.m. Foreign Office states barley situation growing worse and absolute embargo may be laid down. German importers have decided not to take up documents on arriving shipments which seems a clear breach of contract. American exporters should at once consider feasibility of sending a competent representative here, possibly from London, with whom Embassy can cooperate in looking after their legal and other interests. If this is communicated to the Department of Agriculture please say SteereRepresentative in Germany of the Department of Agriculture. joins in recommendation.
